covered_details.description: Missing smp_wmb() on ARM: producer writes data then flag without barrier, consumer reads stale data on ARM/POWER
Ring buffer: writer publishes entry then advances tail without barrier, reader on ARM sees advanced tail but stale entry
Dekker-style mutual exclusion fails on ARM: both threads see other's flag as 0
rcu_dereference() without matching rcu_assign_pointer() barrier on ARM: reader sees new pointer but old pointed-to data
RCU publish pattern: writer stores data then stores pointer, reader loads pointer then loads data via dependency
Spinlock unlock on ARM: critical section stores reordered past unlock store without dmb
RISC-V fence.tso insufficient for full barrier: only orders W->R, not all combinations

covered_details.source: ARM Architecture Reference Manual v8, multi-copy atomicity discussion
covered_details.description: IRIW: two readers observe two independent writes in different orders on non-MCA architectures
Write-read causality: thread 0 writes x, thread 1 reads x and writes y, thread 2 reads y then x; can see y=1 but x=0 on ARM

covered_details.source: Lu et al., Learning from Mistakes, ASPLOS 2008, MySQL #12848
covered_details.description: MySQL: double-checked locking without barrier; thread sees partially-constructed object

covered_details.source: Lu et al., Learning from Mistakes, ASPLOS 2008, Apache #21287
covered_details.description: Apache: flag-based synchronization fails on SMP; store to shared state reordered past flag store

covered_details.source: Lu et al., Learning from Mistakes, ASPLOS 2008, Mozilla #133773
covered_details.description: Mozilla: Peterson lock variant fails on x86-64 (SB pattern)

covered_details.source: Boehm, Threads Cannot Be Implemented as a Library, PLDI 2005
covered_details.description: Double-checked locking broken without memory model guarantees; compiler and hardware reorder stores

covered_details.source: Alglave et al., GPU Concurrency, ASPLOS 2015
covered_details.description: GPU message passing: store-store reordering across workgroups without device-scope fence

covered_details.source: Alglave et al., GPU Concurrency, ASPLOS 2015
covered_details.description: GPU store buffer: store-load reordering within workgroup

covered_details.source: Lustig et al., Formal Analysis of NVIDIA PTX, ASPLOS 2019
covered_details.description: PTX scope mismatch: CTA-scope fence insufficient for cross-CTA communication
MPMC queue: x86-safe store ordering broken on ARM; consumer reads new tail but stale data slot
Arena allocation: metadata store reordered past pointer publication on ARM
SequenceChecker: store-load reordering causes false positive race detection on ARM
Lock-free ring buffer: producer/consumer indexes reordered on ARM

covered_details.source: CWE-362: Concurrent Execution with Shared Resource
covered_details.description: TOCTOU race on shared flag: check then act without barrier; reordering breaks atomicity of check-act sequence

covered_details.source: CWE-567: Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in Multithreaded Context
covered_details.description: Writer publishes data without synchronization; reader on weak-memory architecture sees inconsistent state
Lock implementation fails on weak memory: store-load reordering in spinlock allows concurrent critical sections

covered_details.source: Linux kernel: LKMM coherence test suite
covered_details.description: Coherence violation: two stores to same address observed in different orders by two readers
Two threads each write to two shared variables; one possible interleaving violates per-address coherence
Notification: writer sets flag then notifies waiters; on ARM, flag store may be reordered past notification
Load buffering: thread 0 reads y, writes x; thread 1 reads x, writes y; both see initial values
Chase-Lev deque: steal() and push() interact through shared buffer; ARM reordering causes double-steal
Dictionary rehash: new bucket pointer published before all entries migrated; reader sees partial migration state
SkipList node insertion: forward pointers set then height published; reader on ARM may see new height but uninitialized pointers
Lock-free stack push: CAS on head succeeds but new node's data not yet visible to popper on ARM
M-S queue enqueue: new node linked via CAS but value field reordered; dequeuer sees linked node with stale value
Seqlock write side: data stores may be reordered past sequence counter update on ARM

covered_details.source: NVIDIA CUDA Programming Guide, warp divergence
covered_details.description: GPU warp: threads within a warp diverge on conditional; reconvergence point may have inconsistent shared memory view
Vulkan: workgroup-scope barrier insufficient for cross-workgroup data sharing

uncovered_details.source: M. Michael, Safe Memory Reclamation, PODC 2004
uncovered_details.description: ABA problem in lock-free data structures: CAS succeeds on matching value but underlying state has changed
uncovered_details.reason: ABA is a semantic bug, not a memory ordering issue; not expressible as a litmus test

uncovered_details.description: Livelock: threads make progress but never complete; related to CAS retry loops
uncovered_details.reason: Liveness property, not safety; litmus tests check safety (reachability of forbidden outcomes)

uncovered_details.description: Priority inversion: low-priority thread holds resource needed by high-priority thread
uncovered_details.reason: Scheduling bug, not memory ordering; requires modeling priorities and preemption

uncovered_details.description: False sharing: independent variables on same cache line cause unnecessary invalidation traffic
uncovered_details.reason: Performance issue, not correctness; not expressible as a forbidden outcome

uncovered_details.description: Seqlock reader sees inconsistent data when writer increments sequence counter with wrap-around during long read
uncovered_details.reason: Involves arithmetic overflow, not memory ordering; requires modeling counter semantics

uncovered_details.description: Signal handler accesses data concurrently with main thread; non-atomic access produces torn reads
uncovered_details.reason: Signal-based concurrency requires asynchronous interrupt modeling, not litmus test ordering

note: Coverage is against documented memory-ordering bugs only. Uncovered bugs are out of scope (liveness, scheduling, ABA, signal handling, performance) — not expressible as litmus tests.
